summary: Rewrote scroll behavior in HealthRecordsSection. Removed useLocation import and the hash-based useEffect that collapsed all accordions. Replaced with three effects: (1) mount-only effect reading window.location.hash for deep-link support, opening the target section additively and scrolling with 24px offset; (2) IntersectionObserver scroll-spy reporting the visible section id to setHealthActiveSection via rootMargin '-20% 0px -70% 0px'; (3) registerScrollToHealthSection registration effect that installs the open-and-scroll callback for sub-nav clicks. Added setHealthActiveSection and registerScrollToHealthSection to the context destructure.
notes_for_reviewer: TSC not run per task instructions — Task 3 must complete before a clean compile is possible since context.ts and PetDetail.tsx changes from Task 1 are prerequisites for the new context fields consumed here.
The mount-only hash effect uses window.location.hash (not location from useLocation) to avoid the react-router-dom dependency entirely.
openSections default (new Set(['conditions', 'allergies'])) and all onToggle handlers are unchanged.
